pr.title: gr Shrineroute and Kinokoroute matches, function renaming
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched two Shrine Route stage functions and improved Kinoko Route while also applying broad function renames for common ground and baselib GObj process helpers. The decomp.dev report credited +316 matched bytes and two new matches: grShrineRoute_8020AD58 and grShrineRoute_8020B020; grKinokoRoute_8020754C improved to 99.97% and its .data improved to 33.47%. The large diff is dominated by mechanical callsite renames from Ground_801C14D0 to Ground_GetStageGObj and HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 to HSD_GObj_SetupProc plus include/order/formatting cleanup across many systems.

change_classification.primary_type: stage decomp matching and symbol renaming

what_changed: Implemented grShrineRoute_8020AD58 with a typed signature void grShrineRoute_8020AD58(Ground*, int, CollData*, int, int), using CollData flags, ftLib_80086BE0, Player_GetEntity, Player_GetEntityAtIndex, and ftLib_8008731C to set shrineroute ground vars xCA and xCC.
Implemented grShrineRoute_8020B020 with a typed HSD_GObj-based signature, iterating six entries and toggling JOBJ_HIDDEN via HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll/HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll using gp->gv.shrineroute2.x108.

Updated grShrineroute_GroundVars2 in src/melee/gr/types.h to expose the HSD_GObj* x108 field at the offset needed by grShrineRoute_8020B020, replacing part of a padding blob while preserving later offsets.
Implemented grKinokoRoute_8020754C as an HSD_GObj* returning stage-object setup function and added the grNKr_803E57F0 StageCallbacks table in grkinokoroute.c.
Changed grKinokoRoute_8020754C's header prototype from void-returning to HSD_GObj* returning.
Renamed Ground_801C14D0 to Ground_GetStageGObj in config/GALE01/symbols.txt and propagated the callsite rename through many stage constructors.

Renamed HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 to HSD_GObj_SetupProc in config/GALE01/symbols.txt and propagated the callsite rename across camera, effects, fighter, game mode, ground, item, menu, interface, and other code.
Marked grBb_Route_803E623C as data:string in symbols.txt.
Performed widespread include reordering and formatting-only cleanup in many touched files, which accounts for much of the 191-file diff.
smart_moves: Used high-level semantic names for two very common helpers, Ground_GetStageGObj and HSD_GObj_SetupProc, making repeated stage-object setup patterns much easier to identify and reuse.
Matched Shrine Route behavior by typing the collision callback arguments enough to use CollData, player entity helpers, and ground vars instead of leaving an UNK_PARAMS placeholder.
Exposed only the newly understood x108 field inside grShrineroute_GroundVars2 while retaining explicit padding around it, minimizing risk to later known offsets.

Reconstructed Kinoko Route's setup function around the standard StageCallbacks pattern: get the stage GObj, clear callbacks, install grDisplay_801C5DB0 GX link, copy callback3, call callback0, and register callback2 as a process.
Kept route headers synchronized with newly typed function signatures, avoiding hidden ABI drift between C files and declarations.
Applied symbol renames globally in one PR so future decomp work can search for meaningful setup helpers rather than address-based names.
decomp_lessons: Stage route setup functions often share a recognizable template: StageCallbacks table lookup, Ground_GetStageGObj, user_data as Ground, callback initialization, GObj_SetupGXLink, optional callback0 invocation, and HSD_GObj_SetupProc for callback2.
When a matched function needs a field in an otherwise unknown ground-var struct, split padding just enough to name the field and preserve all subsequent offsets.
Common baselib and ground helper renames can unlock readability across unrelated systems because the same GObj process setup idiom appears in fighters, effects, camera, menus, and stages.
A PR titled as multiple matches may include partial improvements; here decomp.dev reported Shrine Route functions as 100% matched, while Kinoko Route function grKinokoRoute_8020754C was only 99.97%.
Large decomp PRs with many callsite renames should separate semantic changes in the summary because raw added/deleted counts are inflated by mechanical renames and formatting.
naming_conventions: Ground_801C14D0 -> Ground_GetStageGObj for the stage GObj lookup helper.
HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 -> HSD_GObj_SetupProc for registering a GObj process callback with a priority/order argument.
Route-stage callback tables use stage-prefixed data names such as grNKr_803E57F0 for Kinoko Route and grSh_Route_803E58F0 for Shrine Route.
Newly understood ground variable fields retain offset-style names such as x108 when purpose is still unknown.
Stage functions continue to use module-prefixed names with addresses, such as grShrineRoute_8020AD58 and grKinokoRoute_8020754C, until stronger semantic names are known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(0x10) in grShrineRoute_8020AD58 to satisfy stack/layout requirements for matching.
Preserved pointer/offset-sensitive struct layout by replacing padding with HSD_GObj* x108 and recalculating adjacent padding arrays.
Used the standard GET_GROUND and GET_JOBJ macros where they matched expected access patterns.
For grShrineRoute_8020B020, advanced through related Ground records with gp = (Ground*) &gp->gobj, an unusual pointer step that appears evidence-grounded in the matched source.
Added includes only as needed for newly typed calls and macros, including ft/ftlib.h, pl/player.h, baselib/jobj.h, gr/grdisplay.h, and baselib GObj headers.
review_feedback: No substantive human review comments were present in the slice; the only review record shown was an empty dismissed review by ribbanya.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 57.96% (+0.01%, +316 bytes).
decomp.dev bot reported two new 100% matches: main/melee/gr/grshrineroute grShrineRoute_8020AD58 (+176 bytes) and grShrineRoute_8020B020 (+140 bytes).
decomp.dev bot reported improvements, not full matches, for main/melee/gr/grkinokoroute grKinokoRoute_8020754C (+231 bytes, 99.97%) and .data (+80 bytes, 33.47%).
follow_up_queries: Find other route-stage setup functions still using address-based names around StageCallbacks and Ground_GetStageGObj.
Investigate why grKinokoRoute_8020754C remained at 99.97% instead of a full match.
Search for remaining HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54 or Ground_801C14D0 references after this rename.
Refine the semantics of grShrineroute_GroundVars2.x108 and the xCA/xCC flags set by grShrineRoute_8020AD58.
Audit grBb_Route_803E623C and neighboring route data now that it is marked data:string.
